signals.headline: Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire Indefinitely | YES | HIGH**
signals.raw:
Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire Indefinitely | YES | HIGH**  
Trump's indefinite extension of the Iran ceasefire on April 22, 2026, removed geopolitical risk premiums (e.g., Strait of Hormuz tensions), driving a Bitcoin breakout above $76,000 to $77,541 (+2.2% daily, +4.3% weekly) and enabling short unwinds.[1]
matched_markets: Will Trump agree to Iranian enrichment of uranium in April?
Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?
Will Trump name "Elon" or "Musk" in April?

signals.headline: Strategy Discloses $2
signals.raw:
Strategy Discloses $2.54B Bitcoin Purchase | YES | HIGH**  
MicroStrategy revealed a $2.54 billion BTC buy on April 22, 2026, pushing holdings past 815,000 coins and absorbing ~3x April's global miner supply, fueling the rally alongside ETF inflows (e.g., BlackRock's IBIT leading $411M+ streak).[1][2]

signals.headline: Ethereum Tracks BTC Gains to $2,390 | YES | MED**
signals.raw:
Ethereum Tracks BTC Gains to $2,390 | YES | MED**  
Ethereum rose 0.7% to $2,390 on April 22 amid risk-on sentiment from the ceasefire and inflows, holding $2,260–$2,350 range with potential breakout above $2,400 pivot, though lagging BTC.[1][2]
